SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.safety.vacuum

Class VacuumCollapseAnalyzer

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.vacuum.VacuumCollapseAnalyzer
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class VacuumCollapseAnalyzer extends Object implements Serializable
Vacuum-collapse (external-pressure / implosion) screening for a blocked-in vessel that cools.

Implements the HAZOP "Less Pressure" deviation caused by cooling or condensation of a blocked-in (rigid, fixed-volume) vapour space. When a vessel is isolated full of vapour and then loses heat — ambient cooling after shutdown, contact with a cold medium, condensing steam, or hot vapour drawn into a cold line — the vapour contracts and condenses at constant volume, so the internal pressure falls. If the internal pressure drops below the vessel external-pressure (vacuum) rating the shell can buckle/implode.

The analysis performs a constant-volume cooling sweep with NeqSim TVflash: the blocked-in inventory is held at the fixed vessel volume while the temperature is reduced from the initial state to the cold-end temperature. At each step the equilibrium internal pressure (including any vapour condensation) is computed. The minimum internal pressure is compared with atmospheric pressure (to quantify the vacuum) and with the vessel external-pressure rating (the verdict). A screening-level make-up (pad-gas / vacuum-breaker) requirement is estimated to keep the vessel above a chosen set point.

This is a screening calculation. It assumes a rigid vessel, a closed (blocked-in) inventory with no in-breathing, and thermodynamic equilibrium at each temperature. It does not size the vacuum-relief device transient; the make-up estimate is an ideal-gas inventory difference for orientation only.

References:

  • API STD 521 — Pressure-relieving and Depressuring Systems (vacuum / external pressure cases)
  • API STD 2000 — Venting Atmospheric and Low-pressure Storage Tanks (thermal in-breathing)
  • ASME Section VIII Div. 1, UG-28 — design for external pressure
  • IEC 61882 — HAZOP "Less Pressure" deviation
Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Constructor Details

    • VacuumCollapseAnalyzer

      public VacuumCollapseAnalyzer(SystemInterface fluid, double vesselVolumeM3)
      Construct a vacuum-collapse analyzer.
      Parameters:
      fluid - blocked-in inventory; must already have components and a mixing rule configured
      vesselVolumeM3 - rigid internal vessel volume in m3; must be positive
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the fluid is null or the volume is non-positive

  • Method Details

    • setInitialConditions

      public VacuumCollapseAnalyzer setInitialConditions(double pressure, String pressureUnit, double temperature, String temperatureUnit)
      Sets the initial blocked-in pressure and temperature.
      Parameters:
      pressure - pressure value in the supplied unit; must be positive
      pressureUnit - pressure unit supported by PressureUnit, for example bara or barg
      temperature - temperature value in the supplied unit
      temperatureUnit - temperature unit supported by TemperatureUnit, for example K or C
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if pressure or temperature is non-physical

    • setColdEndTemperature

      public VacuumCollapseAnalyzer setColdEndTemperature(double temperature, String temperatureUnit)
      Sets the cold-end temperature the blocked-in vessel cools down to.
      Parameters:
      temperature - temperature value in the supplied unit
      temperatureUnit - temperature unit supported by TemperatureUnit, for example K or C
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the temperature is non-physical

    • setExternalPressureRating

      public VacuumCollapseAnalyzer setExternalPressureRating(double pressure, String pressureUnit)
      Sets the vessel external-pressure (vacuum) rating: the lowest absolute internal pressure the shell tolerates. Use 0 bara for a full-vacuum-rated vessel.
      Parameters:
      pressure - rating value in the supplied unit; must be in [0, atmospheric]
      pressureUnit - pressure unit supported by PressureUnit
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the rating is negative

    • setAtmosphericPressure

      public VacuumCollapseAnalyzer setAtmosphericPressure(double pressure, String pressureUnit)
      Sets the atmospheric reference pressure used to quantify the vacuum depth.
      Parameters:
      pressure - atmospheric pressure value in the supplied unit; must be positive
      pressureUnit - pressure unit supported by PressureUnit
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the pressure is non-positive

    • setMakeupSetpoint

      public VacuumCollapseAnalyzer setMakeupSetpoint(double pressure, String pressureUnit)
      Sets the make-up / pad-gas set point: the internal pressure that vacuum protection should restore.
      Parameters:
      pressure - set-point value in the supplied unit; must be positive
      pressureUnit - pressure unit supported by PressureUnit
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the pressure is non-positive

    • setMakeupGasMolarMass

      public VacuumCollapseAnalyzer setMakeupGasMolarMass(double molarMassKgPerMol)
      Sets the molar mass of the make-up gas used for the screening pad-gas estimate (default nitrogen).
      Parameters:
      molarMassKgPerMol - make-up gas molar mass in kg/mol; must be positive
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the molar mass is non-positive

    • setCoolingSteps

      public VacuumCollapseAnalyzer setCoolingSteps(int steps)
      Sets the number of temperature steps in the cooling sweep.
      Parameters:
      steps - number of cooling steps; must be at least 1
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if steps is less than 1

    • analyze

      public VacuumCollapseResult analyze()
      Runs the constant-volume cooling sweep and evaluates the vacuum-collapse verdict.
      Returns:
      the vacuum-collapse result
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if required setup is missing or the cold-end temperature is not below the initial temperature

    • vaporMoleFraction

      private double vaporMoleFraction(SystemInterface state)
      Returns the vapour mole fraction of the current state.
      Parameters:
      state - the fluid state after a flash
      Returns:
      vapour mole fraction in [0, 1]; 0 when no gas phase is present

    • scaleMoles

      private void scaleMoles(SystemInterface state, double factor)
      Scales the absolute inventory of the closed system by the given factor while preserving composition.

      Implemented as per-component mole additions because setTotalNumberOfMoles only sets the scalar total and leaves the component moles unchanged, corrupting the average molar mass and density.

      Parameters:
      state - the fluid state to scale
      factor - multiplicative scaling factor; values <= 0, NaN, or equal to 1 are ignored
